category: ERP Test / Scenario / UI / API / Report Generation

items.category: ERP Test / Scenario / UI / API / Report Generation
items.title: Test pattern: accounting balance invariant
items.description: Generate a test that posts a sample voucher and asserts sum(debits) == sum(credits), and that the trial balance still nets to zero afterward. Run it for every posting document type the generator emits.
items.applicability: Automated test suite for any generated accounting module.
items.example: Post a 1000 sales invoice; assert Dr 1000 == Cr 1000 and total-debit-balances == total-credit-balances.
items.limitation: Balance invariance proves arithmetic, not correct account mapping; pair it with assertions on the specific accounts hit.

items.category: ERP Test / Scenario / UI / API / Report Generation
items.title: Test pattern: negative-stock rejection
items.description: Generate a test that attempts to issue more stock than is on hand and asserts the operation is rejected with a domain error and that no stock-ledger row was written and the balance is unchanged.
items.applicability: Automated test suite for inventory issue paths.
items.example: Warehouse holds 8; issue 10; assert DomainException, ledger row count unchanged, balance still 8.
items.limitation: The test must assert no side effect occurred, not merely that an exception was thrown; a half-applied movement that still throws is a real bug this catches only with the side-effect assertion.

items.category: ERP Test / Scenario / UI / API / Report Generation
items.title: Test pattern: maker-checker negative path
items.description: Generate a test that has the same user both create and approve a document requiring maker-checker, and asserts approval is rejected. Also assert that an unapproved document cannot post ledger/stock impact.
items.applicability: Automated test suite for segregation-of-duties controls.
items.example: User A creates JE-001 and attempts to approve it; assert rejection 'maker cannot be checker'; JE remains unposted.
items.limitation: Tests the control mechanism, not the approval-matrix configuration; matrix thresholds need their own value-boundary tests.

items.category: ERP Test / Scenario / UI / API / Report Generation
items.title: Test pattern: RBAC negative path
items.description: Generate a test that calls a protected action/endpoint as a user lacking the required role and asserts 403/forbidden and that no state changed. Cover both UI route guards and API authorization for the same capability.
items.applicability: Automated test suite for role-based access control.
items.example: Read-only user POSTs to /api/journal-entries; assert 403 and no journal row created.
items.limitation: A passing negative RBAC test does not prove the positive path is correctly scoped; pair with a positive test that the authorised role succeeds.

items.category: ERP Test / Scenario / UI / API / Report Generation
items.title: Test pattern: posted-document immutability
items.description: Generate a test that submits a document then attempts to edit or delete it and asserts the attempt is blocked. Assert that correction is only possible via cancellation/reversal.
items.applicability: Automated test suite for document-lifecycle controls.
items.example: Submit SINV-001; attempt PUT to change amount; assert rejected; ledger untouched; only Cancel is offered.
items.limitation: Immutability of accounting fields may coexist with editable metadata; the test must target ledger-affecting fields specifically to avoid false failures on amendable remarks.

items.category: ERP Test / Scenario / UI / API / Report Generation
items.title: Test pattern: append-only audit trail
items.description: Generate a test that performs a create/update/delete and asserts an audit record was written capturing actor, action, entity, timestamp and before/after, and that audit rows cannot themselves be edited or deleted.
items.applicability: Automated test suite for auditability controls.
items.example: Approve a payment; assert an audit row {user, 'approve', PE-001, ts} exists and an attempt to delete it is rejected.
items.limitation: Audit-trail tests prove capture, not completeness of coverage; a separate review must confirm every sensitive action is actually instrumented.

items.category: ERP Test / Scenario / UI / API / Report Generation
items.title: Test pattern: P&L and Balance Sheet consistency
items.description: Generate a test over a sample dataset that asserts Balance Sheet Assets == Liabilities + Equity, and that Equity includes the P&L net profit for the same period, so the two statements reconcile.
items.applicability: Automated test suite for financial-statement correctness.
items.example: Seed transactions; assert Assets 150000 == Liabilities 100000 + Equity 50000 and Equity contains net profit 50000.
items.limitation: Consistency between statements does not validate classification of individual accounts; misclassified-but-consistent data still passes, so account-mapping tests remain necessary.

items.category: ERP Test / Scenario / UI / API / Report Generation
items.title: Test pattern: correction-by-reversal equivalence
items.description: Generate a test that posts a document, cancels it, and asserts the net GL and stock impact is exactly zero (original + reversal == 0), proving cancellation fully unwinds all side effects.
items.applicability: Automated test suite for the reversal/cancellation mechanism.
items.example: Post then cancel a delivery note; assert net stock change 0 and net GL change 0 across all affected accounts.
items.limitation: Zero-net at original rate is required; if valuation drifted between post and cancel, a residual remains and the test must use the original valuation, which the generator must preserve.

items.category: ERP Test / Scenario / UI / API / Report Generation
items.title: Playwright scenario: end-to-end order-to-cash happy path
items.description: Generate a Playwright spec that drives the UI through quotation -> sales order -> delivery -> invoice -> receipt, asserting each document submits, the next is pre-filled from the prior, and final outstanding is zero. Use stable selectors (data-testid), not text.
items.applicability: Browser end-to-end suite validating the full sales flow through the real UI.
items.example: Playwright: create QTN, convert to SO, deliver, invoice, receive payment; assert invoice outstanding shows 0.
items.limitation: E2E specs are slow and brittle; keep them few and high-value, gate selectors on data-testid, and rely on unit/integration tests for breadth rather than driving every case through the browser.

items.category: ERP Test / Scenario / UI / API / Report Generation
items.title: Playwright scenario: negative-path validation in the UI
items.description: Generate a Playwright spec that submits an invalid document (unbalanced journal, over-issue stock) and asserts the UI shows the domain error, stays on the form, and persists nothing. Confirms server validation surfaces correctly to the user.
items.applicability: Browser end-to-end suite for validation feedback.
items.example: Playwright: enter a journal with Dr 1000 / Cr 900, submit; assert visible 'not balanced' error and no new JE in the list.
items.limitation: UI negative tests confirm the message reaches the user but should not replace fast server-side validation tests; duplicating every validation rule at the browser level is wasteful.

items.category: ERP Test / Scenario / UI / API / Report Generation
items.title: UI rule: generate List / Detail / Create / Edit pages per entity
items.description: For each entity emit four views: a List (paged table of lightweight columns), a Detail (read-only full record), a Create form, and an Edit form (Draft only). Submitted documents show Detail with Cancel, not Edit, honouring the immutability rule.
items.applicability: UI scaffolding generated for every transactional and master entity.
items.example: Sales Invoice: /list (paged), /{id} detail with Cancel, /create form, /{id}/edit only when Draft.
items.limitation: List views must select only lightweight columns and never materialise large text/content fields, or large datasets hang the page — mirrors the repository's list-query rule.

items.category: ERP Test / Scenario / UI / API / Report Generation
items.title: UI rule: list views need search, filter, sort, pagination server-side
items.description: Generate list pages with server-side search (indexed fields), column filters, sortable columns and pagination. Never fetch the full table to the client; apply skip/take and order in the query so the page is bounded regardless of table size.
items.applicability: UI scaffolding for every list/report view.
items.example: Sales Invoice list: filter status=Submitted, search customer, sort by date desc, page size 50, server-paged.
items.limitation: Client-side paging of a fully-loaded table appears to work on small data and fails silently at scale; the cut must be in the database query, not the browser.

items.category: ERP Test / Scenario / UI / API / Report Generation
items.title: UI rule: status badges and lifecycle affordances
items.description: Render document status as a colour-coded badge (e.g. Draft grey, Submitted green, Cancelled red) and only show actions valid for the current state (Submit on Draft, Cancel on Submitted). This makes the state machine visible and prevents invalid-action clicks.
items.applicability: UI scaffolding for all stateful documents.
items.example: A Submitted invoice shows a green 'Submitted' badge, a Cancel button, and no Edit/Submit buttons.
items.limitation: Hiding invalid actions improves UX but is not a security control; the server must still reject invalid transitions regardless of what the UI exposes.

items.category: ERP Test / Scenario / UI / API / Report Generation
items.title: API rule: generate GET (list+by-id) and POST per entity
items.description: For each entity emit REST endpoints: GET /entities (paged, filterable), GET /entities/{id}, and POST /entities (create). Mutating posted documents uses POST /entities/{id}/cancel rather than PUT/DELETE, consistent with immutability.
items.applicability: REST API scaffolding generated alongside the UI for every entity.
items.example: GET /api/sales-invoices?status=Submitted&page=1; GET /api/sales-invoices/42; POST /api/sales-invoices; POST /api/sales-invoices/42/cancel.
items.limitation: A generated CRUD surface is not automatically safe; each endpoint needs authorization, validation and rate-limiting added — the route shape alone is insufficient.

items.category: ERP Test / Scenario / UI / API / Report Generation
items.title: API rule: map DomainException to HTTP 400 with a problem body
items.description: Generate a global exception filter that maps domain/validation errors to HTTP 400 with a structured problem-details body (code, message, field), maps not-found to 404, unauthorized to 401/403, and unexpected errors to 500 without leaking internals.
items.applicability: REST API error-handling scaffolding shared across all endpoints.
items.example: POST an unbalanced journal -> 400 {code:'VOUCHER_UNBALANCED', message:'debits != credits'}; never a 500 stack trace.
items.limitation: Returning 400 for true server faults hides bugs; the mapping must distinguish client-correctable validation from server errors, and must not echo stack traces to clients.

items.category: ERP Test / Scenario / UI / API / Report Generation
items.title: API rule: validate request DTOs before touching the domain
items.description: Generate request DTOs with declarative validation (required, ranges, formats, referential existence) applied before the domain service runs, so malformed input is rejected at the edge with 400 and never reaches posting logic.
items.applicability: REST API input-validation scaffolding for every write endpoint.
items.example: POST sales invoice with qty -1 -> 400 'qty must be positive' before any ledger logic executes.
items.limitation: Edge validation does not replace domain invariants (balance, stock); both layers are needed, since edge rules cannot express cross-entity business rules.

items.category: ERP Test / Scenario / UI / API / Report Generation
items.title: Report rule: generate General Ledger and account-statement reports
items.description: Emit a General Ledger report listing every posting line for an account over a period with running balance, and a party statement (customer/supplier ledger) showing invoices, payments and outstanding. Source from GL movement, paged and filterable by account/party/date.
items.applicability: Reporting scaffolding over the accounting core.
items.example: GL for 'Bank' 2026-05: opening 10000, +/- movements, closing 12000 with running balance per row.
items.limitation: Reports must read consistently as-of a point; running over live data during posting can show transient imbalances, so reports should pin to a consistent snapshot or read-committed view.

items.category: ERP Test / Scenario / UI / API / Report Generation
items.title: Report rule: generate Trial Balance, P&L and Balance Sheet
items.description: Emit the three core financial statements from GL balances: Trial Balance (per-account debit/credit, totals equal), Profit & Loss (period income/expense, net profit), Balance Sheet (assets = liabilities + equity as-of). Each must reconcile to the others.
items.applicability: Financial-reporting scaffolding required of any generated ERP.
items.example: One click produces TB (balanced), P&L (net profit 50000) and BS (Assets == Liab + Equity incl. 50000).
items.limitation: These reports reflect what was posted; they cannot detect misclassification or missing accruals, so a clean set of statements is not an assurance of correctness.

items.category: ERP Test / Scenario / UI / API / Report Generation
items.title: Report rule: generate AR/AP ageing and Stock Balance reports
items.description: Emit AR and AP ageing reports bucketing outstanding by due-date age (current/30/60/90+), and a Stock Balance report of qty and value per item/warehouse as-of a date, sourced from the stock ledger and reconciled to the inventory GL account.
items.applicability: Operational-reporting scaffolding over receivables, payables and inventory.
items.example: AR ageing: ACME 1150 in the 30-day bucket; Stock Balance: RawX WH-Main qty 48 value 2400.
items.limitation: Ageing 'as-of' and bucket boundaries are policy and must match the accounting definition; stock value in the report must tie to the GL or one of them is wrong.

items.category: ERP Test / Scenario / UI / API / Report Generation
items.title: Dashboard rule: generate KPI tiles from bounded aggregate queries
items.description: Emit dashboard tiles (receivables outstanding, payables outstanding, monthly sales, stock value) backed by simple, indexed aggregate queries with separate counts, never group-by-constant or full-table scans of heavy columns. Cache snapshots where the value tolerates slight staleness.
items.applicability: Dashboard scaffolding; the landing summary of a generated ERP.
items.example: Tile 'Outstanding AR' = SUM(outstanding) over Submitted sales invoices, computed by an indexed query, refreshed periodically.
items.limitation: Dashboard aggregates on the request path are a classic source of page hangs; expensive or clever aggregations must be precomputed/cached, mirroring this repository's dashboard rules.

items.category: ERP Test / Scenario / UI / API / Report Generation
items.title: Scenario spec: month-end close runs and locks the period
items.description: Real-life scenario as a test spec: accountant posts accruals, reconciles control accounts, confirms trial balance is balanced, then locks the period; assert that a back-dated posting into the locked period is rejected and that reopening requires an authorised role and writes an audit row.
items.applicability: Acceptance scenario validating the period-close control end to end.
items.example: Close May, then attempt a 2026-05-20 journal -> rejected 'period locked'; reopen as Controller -> audit row written.
items.limitation: The scenario validates the lock mechanism, not the completeness of the close checklist; a locked period can still be missing accruals the checklist did not enforce.

items.category: ERP Test / Scenario / UI / API / Report Generation
items.title: Scenario spec: oversell prevention on concurrent orders
items.description: Real-life scenario: two sales orders for the last units of an item are confirmed concurrently; assert that available-to-promise prevents committing more than on-hand, one order is flagged short, and no negative stock results after both deliveries attempt to post.
items.applicability: Acceptance scenario for inventory availability under concurrency.
items.example: On-hand 5; SO-A reserves 5, SO-B for 3 flagged short; delivery of SO-B blocked; stock never goes negative.
items.limitation: Correct behaviour depends on the reservation/ATP design and on transactional isolation; without proper locking the race can still oversell, which is exactly what this scenario must stress.

items.category: ERP Test / Scenario / UI / API / Report Generation
items.title: Scenario spec: customer return with credit note and restock
items.description: Real-life scenario: a customer returns goods; the clerk raises a credit note against the original invoice; assert revenue and receivable reverse, returned stock is restocked at original valuation, the original invoice links to the credit note, and the customer outstanding reduces correctly.
items.applicability: Acceptance scenario for the sales-return flow.
items.example: Return 2 of 10 WidgetA: credit note reverses 230, stock +2 at original rate, invoice outstanding reduced by 230.
items.limitation: Restock at original valuation only holds if the item is returned to the same warehouse and condition; damaged-goods or restocking-fee handling is a separate policy not covered.

items.category: ERP Test / Scenario / UI / API / Report Generation
items.title: Scenario spec: three-way match blocks an over-billed supplier invoice
items.description: Real-life scenario: a supplier invoice bills more than the PO/receipt quantity; assert three-way match (PO vs receipt vs invoice) flags the variance and blocks or holds the invoice from posting until approved, preventing overpayment.
items.applicability: Acceptance scenario for procure-to-pay control.
items.example: PO 100, received 100, invoice for 110 -> match variance +10 flagged; invoice held pending approval, not auto-posted.
items.limitation: Three-way match requires all three documents to exist and be linked; service or expense invoices without a receipt fall outside it and need a different approval control.

items.category: ERP Test / Scenario / UI / API / Report Generation
items.title: Scenario spec: maker-checker on a high-value payment
items.description: Real-life scenario: a payment above an approval threshold is created by a clerk and must be approved by a different authorised user before posting; assert the maker cannot approve, the payment stays unposted until a valid checker approves, and the approval is audited.
items.applicability: Acceptance scenario for segregation-of-duties on disbursements.
items.example: Clerk creates a 50000 supplier payment; self-approval rejected; Manager approves; only then Dr AP / Cr Bank posts.
items.limitation: Threshold and required approver role are configuration; the scenario validates enforcement at one threshold and must be re-run at boundary values to confirm the matrix is wired correctly.

items.category: ERP Test / Scenario / UI / API / Report Generation
items.title: Scenario spec: stock transfer keeps total value constant
items.description: Real-life scenario: goods are transferred between warehouses; assert source decreases and target increases by the same qty and value, total on-hand and total inventory value across warehouses are unchanged, and per-warehouse balances are correct afterward.
items.applicability: Acceptance scenario for inter-warehouse movement integrity.
items.example: Transfer 20 RawX Main->North at 50: Main -20/-1000, North +20/+1000, total value unchanged at all times.
items.limitation: Value-neutrality assumes one inventory account; if warehouses map to different GL accounts a reclass posts and the scenario assertion must account for it.

items.category: ERP Test / Scenario / UI / API / Report Generation
items.title: Scenario spec: fiscal-year close zeroes P&L into retained earnings
items.description: Real-life scenario: at year end the system posts a closing entry transferring all income and expense balances to retained earnings; assert all P&L accounts read zero at the new year's opening, balance-sheet balances carry forward, and the next year's opening balance sheet still balances.
items.applicability: Acceptance scenario for the annual-close routine.
items.example: FY2026 net profit 50000 closed to Retained Earnings; FY2027 opens with P&L accounts at 0 and BS carried forward, Assets == Liab + Equity.
items.limitation: Only nominal accounts must zero; the scenario must explicitly assert that real (balance-sheet) accounts did NOT reset, which is the most common year-close defect.
